name: Chandigarh
landmark: Nek Chand's Rock Garden and Le Corbusier's Capitol Complex
stopoverNote: Chandigarh Airport (IXC) and Chandigarh Junction connect the city to Delhi, Mumbai and hill-state gateways; inside the UT, sector-grid distances are short and app cabs/autos work well. Capitol Complex is a security-sensitive UNESCO site: book/confirm the fixed guided tour slot in advance, carry original photo ID and expect bag restrictions.

diet.vegetarian: Chandigarh is very vegetarian-friendly: Punjabi staples such as chole bhature, paneer tikka, dal makhani, aloo paratha, kulcha, rajma-chawal and lassi are easy around Sector 17, Sector 35 and Sukhna Lake cafés. Pure-veg sweets/restaurants are common, and most hotel breakfasts have vegetarian options.
diet.vegan: Vegan eating is possible in urban cafés and simple dhabas but needs clear requests because Punjabi food often uses ghee, butter, cream, curd, paneer and dairy-based lassi. Ask for oil-only cooking, plain roti without butter, chole/rajma without ghee tadka, and skip paneer, curd, cream gravies and sweets unless confirmed dairy-free.
phrases: Mujhe shakahari khana chahiye, bina meat aur anda = I want vegetarian food, without meat or egg
Kripya ghee, makkhan, dahi, doodh aur paneer na daalein = please do not add ghee, butter, curd, milk or paneer
Sirf tel mein bana dena, dairy nahi = please cook only in oil, no dairy

links.label: Government Museum and Art Gallery — Chandigarh Tourism
The Architectural Work of Le Corbusier — UNESCO

cities.name: Chandigarh
cities.notes: Compact planned city base for Sector 1's Capitol Complex, Rock Garden and Sukhna Lake, plus the museums, gardens and markets around Sectors 10, 16 and 17.

cities.name: Chandigarh & around
cities.notes: Optional day-trip base for families or slower travellers adding ChhatBir Zoo near Zirakpur and the Mughal-style Pinjore Gardens north of the city.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel 4G/5G coverage is strong across Chandigarh, Sector 17, Sukhna Lake and the airport/railway station; coverage is also reliable toward Zirakpur and Pinjore, but download maps for hill-road extensions beyond Morni or into Himachal.

Chandigarh.name: Chandigarh
Chandigarh.note: Two days covers the feasible city core: Sector 1's Capitol-Rock Garden-Sukhna cluster, then the museum-garden-market cluster across Sectors 10, 16 and 17. Add the third day only for a slower architecture/museum pace or bad summer heat breaks.

days.theme: Sector 1 icons: Capitol, Rock Garden and Sukhna Lake
days.note: Keep this as a tight Sector 1 day: distances are short, but the Capitol Complex is only possible with a confirmed guided slot and security checks. Carry original photo ID and avoid large bags.

activities.name: Capitol Complex guided UNESCO tour
activities.tip: Fixed guided slots are commonly around 10am, 12pm and 3pm; register/confirm with Chandigarh Tourism, arrive early and carry original photo ID. Access can change on government working days and holidays.

activities.name: Open Hand Monument and High Court exterior
activities.tip: Best paired with the official Capitol tour because the complex is security controlled.

activities.name: Nek Chand's Rock Garden
activities.tip: Official 2026 Chandigarh Tourism listing shows these fees; go before late afternoon in summer and keep water handy.

activities.name: Sukhna Lake promenade and Garden of Silence
activities.cost: Free; boating varies, verify at counter
activities.tip: Entry is free on the official listing; boating rates change by boat type, so confirm locally before paying. Sunset is the most comfortable slot.

meals: Paneer tikka or dal makhani dinner
Sweet or salted lassi if you take dairy

days.theme: Museums, Rose Garden and Sector 17 plaza
days.note: This day clusters the western/central sectors: start indoors at Sector 10, move to the open gardens when it is cooler, and finish with the pedestrian Sector 17 plaza for dinner and shopping.

activities.name: Government Museum and Art Gallery
activities.tip: Official museum listing shows Tuesday–Sunday opening and Monday closure; confirm holiday closures before visiting.

activities.name: Le Corbusier Centre or Architecture Museum
activities.tip: Use this if the Capitol tour was shortened or you want more context on Chandigarh's planned-city design.

activities.name: Zakir Hussain Rose Garden
activities.tip: February–March is the strongest bloom/Rose Festival window; in May–June go early or skip the midday heat.

activities.name: Sector 17 pedestrian plaza
activities.tip: Evenings are liveliest for shopping, fountains and old-school cafés; it is an easy dinner stop after the museum and gardens.

meals: Aloo tikki chaat without curd for vegan travellers

days.note: Use this optional day if summer heat forced long breaks, if the Capitol tour moved to another slot, or if you want a slower museum-and-café pass without padding the core itinerary.

activities.name: International Dolls Museum
activities.tip: Useful for families; verify current timings and ticket at the counter.

activities.name: Leisure Valley and Shanti Kunj walk
activities.tip: Best before 9am or after 5pm outside winter.

activities.name: Sector 35 café and sweets crawl
activities.tip: Good for vegetarian Punjabi food; vegans should clearly ask for no ghee, butter, curd, milk or paneer.

meals: Plain roti with chole for dairy-free travellers

Chandigarh & around.name: Chandigarh & around
Chandigarh & around.note: One day is enough for a family-friendly outer loop. ChhatBir and Pinjore sit in opposite directions from central Chandigarh, so start early, use a private cab, and drop one stop if travelling with small children or in peak summer.

days.theme: ChhatBir Zoo and Pinjore Gardens day trip
days.note: This is a practical taxi day rather than a sector-grid day: ChhatBir Zoo is southeast near Zirakpur and Pinjore Gardens are north near Kalka. Keep lunch flexible and check Monday/holiday closures before leaving.

activities.name: ChhatBir Zoo / Mahendra Chaudhary Zoological Park
activities.tip: Official ticketing is online; the zoo closes Mondays and major national holidays. Verify add-on safari/e-cart prices while booking.

activities.name: Zirakpur or Panchkula lunch break
activities.tip: Choose a simple Punjabi vegetarian restaurant; vegans should ask for oil-only cooking and no dairy.

activities.name: Pinjore / Yadavindra Gardens
activities.tip: Recent public 2026 guides list low adult/child fees, but verify at the Haryana Tourism counter because event and foreigner pricing can differ.

activities.name: Return to Chandigarh via Panchkula
activities.tip: Avoid late-evening hill-road extensions unless continuing toward Himachal.

meals: Paneer paratha with curd if you take dairy
Plain dal-rice and roti for vegan travellers

connections.method: App cab or private taxi day-hire via Zirakpur/Panchkula depending on the chosen outer loop

extras: Capitol Complex is the one non-negotiable timing constraint: book or confirm the official guided tour, carry original government photo ID, arrive early and expect security restrictions on bags and access.

Chandigarh is compact and grid-planned; cluster by sector instead of zig-zagging across town. Sector 1 pairs Capitol Complex, Rock Garden and Sukhna Lake; Sectors 10/16/17 pair museums, Rose Garden and the plaza.
Avoid May–June midday sightseeing because temperatures can approach 40°C; schedule outdoor gardens and lake walks for early morning or sunset.
July–August monsoon showers are usually not mountain-road dangerous inside the city, but they can disrupt lake/garden walks and make the Pinjore/Morni side trip less pleasant.
Chandigarh works as a gateway to Punjab, Haryana and Himachal Pradesh: keep onward hill transfers in daylight and avoid assuming a same-day relaxed hill arrival after a full city sightseeing day.
